files.title: ORIENTATION PACKET
files.subtitle: For Newly Assigned Personnel
files.author: APD-14 Records Division
files.summary: Standard-issue orientation packet. Read this before requesting further access.
files.body:
## Welcome to APD-14

You are viewing the archive terminal for **APD (Anomalous Properties Database), Site 14**, a technology partner formerly under exclusive license to the SCP Foundation. This orientation packet is unrestricted and reissued to every new hire, contractor, and visiting researcher.

### Notice to all personnel

APD's exclusive license with the Foundation is currently suspended following the loss of APD-14 on 2026-06-18. All new hires and visiting personnel should review **FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT** (Level 1 clearance) before beginning any fieldwork. Former Site Director Ryu Hunter remains at large.

### How this terminal works

Each folder you can browse is called a **Directory**. Directories contain **Files** -- lore documents, personnel records, incident logs, and the occasional field simulation. You can move around in three ways, and you never have to use more than one of them:

- **Click or tap** any row to open it.
- **Arrow keys + Enter** to navigate without a mouse.
- **Type commands** at the prompt if you'd rather drive it like a real terminal -- try `help` for the full list.

### Clearance, in plain terms

Every file lists the clearance level required to view it, from **LEVEL 0 (Unrestricted)** up to **LEVEL 5 (Cosmic Top Secret)**. Your session starts at Level 0. If you open something above your level, the terminal will warn you and ask you to confirm before proceeding -- some files allow this override, some don't. Nothing here is a real security system; it's a framing device, so read the warnings for flavor rather than an actual locked door.

You can change your simulated clearance at any time from the status bar, or by typing `clearance` at the prompt.

### A note on redactions

Blocks of `█████` inside a document are intentional. They are not a rendering error, and there is nothing hidden underneath them to reveal.

---

If you're only here to read, the directories below this one are a good place to start. **APD // CORPORATE HISTORY & INCIDENT ARCHIVE** covers what happened in full. If you're APD-14 staff adding new material to this archive, see the README that shipped with this project instead of this packet.

*Secure. Contain. Protect.*

files.title: INTERDEPARTMENTAL JURISDICTION AUDIT
files.subtitle: APD Compliance Review, Conducted Prior to Full License Ratification
files.author: Joint Compliance Review Board (RAISA / ISD / IA)
files.summary: The compliance review conducted before the Foundation extended APD its exclusive license, addressing jurisdictional overlap with RAISA, Internal Security, and the Intelligence Agency.
files.body:
## Purpose

This audit was commissioned ahead of the Foundation's decision to extend APD a full, exclusive licensing agreement. Its purpose was narrow: determine whether APD's proposed scope of work created jurisdictional conflict with any existing Foundation department, and if so, recommend either a revised scope or a denial of the license. This document is not an endorsement of APD as an organization; it addresses structure and jurisdiction only.

## Methodology

Representatives from the Records, Archives, and Information Security Administration (RAISA), the Internal Security Department (ISD), and the Intelligence Agency (IA) were each asked to review APD's proposed scope of work against their own department's mandate and flag any overlap. Findings below are organized by department.

## RAISA Findings

RAISA's mandate is Foundation-wide: it holds sole authority over classification policy, redaction standards, and the Foundation's core archival infrastructure, and it retains final say over what is preserved, what is destroyed, and what is shown to whom. No part of APD's proposed work touches any of this. APD was not offered, and did not request, access to RAISA's classification authority, its backup infrastructure, or its role in determining Foundation-wide archival policy.

What APD proposed instead was narrower and more mechanical: the engineering and maintenance of containment-monitoring hardware and predictive breach-detection software for a single overloaded cluster, Site-19 and its later satellite APD-14, work RAISA's own technical staff acknowledged they lacked the throughput to keep pace with. RAISA remains the policy authority for every system APD builds. APD builds; RAISA governs. The Board found no conflict, provided this boundary is enforced by contract and re-audited annually.

## Internal Security Department Findings

ISD's concern was narrower and more personal: could APD's presence introduce a security or loyalty risk inside the Foundation's own ranks. APD does not investigate Foundation personnel, does not have visibility into Foundation personnel files beyond what is strictly necessary to badge its own staff into shared facilities, and holds no enforcement or disciplinary authority over anyone, Foundation or APD.

ISD's recommendation was procedural rather than jurisdictional: that APD personnel be subject to the same vetting, monitoring, and incident-reporting standards as any other contractor with facility access, and that ISD retain the right to audit APD's own personnel records without notice. This was accepted as a condition of the license. The Board found no jurisdictional conflict; ISD's authority over Foundation security is undiminished, and APD was never proposed as an alternative to it.

## Intelligence Agency Findings

IA's mandate concerns the Foundation's posture toward the world outside its own walls: infiltration, counter-infiltration, and coordination with the Department of External Affairs. APD's work is entirely internal to Foundation-controlled facilities. It does not gather intelligence on other groups of interest, does not coordinate external operations, and has no contact with any organization outside the Foundation in the course of its contracted work. The Board found no conflict, and IA did not request further review.

## On Mobile Task Forces and Delta-Class Units

The most common objection raised internally was not jurisdictional but practical: if Mobile Task Forces already exist to recover anomalies exceeding standard field capacity, why does APD need field units of its own?

The answer is training, not authority. Foundation MTFs are drawn from across the Foundation's own personnel and are, by design, general-purpose: elite, but trained on Foundation equipment and Foundation procedure. None are trained on APD's proprietary detection and monitoring hardware, because that hardware did not exist until APD built it. Cross-training an existing MTF to APD's specification was assessed and rejected as slower and less reliable than simply allowing APD's own engineers, the people who built the equipment, to recover what it detects. APD's Delta-class units exist to close that specific and narrow gap. They hold no authority Foundation MTFs do not already have, they do not compete for the same assignments, and Foundation MTF Commanders retain the standing right to request their assistance, or override their involvement entirely, exactly as they may with any local field unit.

## Determination

The Board found no jurisdictional overlap sufficient to deny APD's license, provided the boundaries above are maintained: RAISA retains all classification and archival policy authority; ISD retains full audit and enforcement authority over APD personnel; IA's external mandate is untouched; and APD's Delta-class units operate strictly within the technical gap Foundation MTFs are not equipped to cover. The license was approved on this basis later that year.

## Addendum

This audit predates the events of June 2026 and the suspension of APD's license. It is preserved for the historical record. Whether the boundaries described above held in practice, particularly with respect to ISD's stated audit authority, is a question this document cannot answer.

files.title: SCP-APD-002
files.author: O5-█
files.summary: Access denied at your current clearance. This entry cannot be overridden.
files.body:
**Item #:** APD-002

**Object Class:** Keter

### Special Containment Procedures

SCP-APD-002 is held in an air-gapped server enclosure in the APD-14 sub-level systems core, physically isolated from any network capable of external connection. ████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████. Personnel assigned to monitoring duty may not serve more than four consecutive hours per rotation and must undergo a full memetic screening before and after each shift.

Under no circumstances is SCP-APD-002 to be connected, even temporarily, to any system that has ever touched the Foundation's or APD's shared archival network.

### Description

SCP-APD-002 presents as a corrupted data structure of unknown origin and indeterminate size, first detected in January 2026 when it began overwriting adjacent records in an APD-14 test archive during a routine migration. SCP-APD-002 does not require active retrieval to spread: any system that reads it, in full or in part, begins exhibiting the same corruption within an average of eleven hours. No recovery method attempted to date has succeeded; affected systems are destroyed rather than sanitized.

Personnel who directly view SCP-APD-002's content, rather than its effects on a host system, report the sensation of remembering something that never happened to them, in increasing detail with prolonged exposure. █████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████.

### Addendum APD-002-1: Recovery

SCP-APD-002 was recovered by an APD Splinter unit after automated monitoring software flagged unexplained corruption spreading through a mirrored backup of Site-19's own archival systems, three relay hops removed from any Foundation-facing network. How it reached that mirror remains under investigation. It is the only Keter-class item ever assigned the SCP-APD- prefix.

### Addendum APD-002-2: Designation Review

Unlike SCP-APD-001, SCP-APD-002's renumbering review has been deferred indefinitely. Given its classification and active containment requirements, Internal Security has determined that reassigning or transferring its file carries more operational risk than any benefit gained from standardizing its designation. It remains SCP-APD-002 for the foreseeable future.

files.title: INCIDENT REPORT 77-Δ
files.subtitle: Minor Containment Breach, Archival Wing B
files.author: Security Officer T. █████
files.summary: A junior researcher held SCP-APD-001 for the full ninety-second interval out of curiosity. No injuries, no breach.
files.body:
**Filed by:** Security Officer T. █████

**Location:** Archival Wing B, Site-19

### Summary

At approximately 02:14, a junior researcher assigned to unrelated cataloguing duties in Archival Wing B removed SCP-APD-001 from its safe without authorization and held it for the full activation interval. The object performed as expected per its standard description. The researcher reported the entry as "accurate and mildly upsetting" and returned the object voluntarily.

### Findings

No anomalous side effects were observed. The safe's lock was found to be non-anomalous and simply left unsecured by the previous shift. Disciplinary action was limited to a formal note in the researcher's file and a mandatory refresher on Form APD-7 procedure.

### Recommendation

Install a keypad lock on the Wing B document safe. Logged as low-severity; no further action required.

files.title: FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT
files.subtitle: RYU HUNTER -- CONSIDERED ARMED AND EXTREMELY DANGEROUS
files.author: Foundation Internal Security Division
files.summary: Widely circulated fugitive bulletin. All personnel are required to review.
files.body:
All personnel are required to review this bulletin in full.

**SUBJECT:** Ryu Hunter

**FORMER POSITION:** Site Director, APD-14 (Anomalous Properties Database)

**STATUS:** At large. Whereabouts unknown since 2026-06-18.

### Charges

Ryu Hunter is wanted in connection with the following, arising from the loss of APD-14 on 2026-06-18:

- Unauthorized activation of a facility self-destruct protocol, resulting in the total loss of APD-14's central systems core.
- The deaths of three sitting Overseer Council members present at APD-14 at the time of the incident.
- Destruction of Foundation-licensed proprietary technology.

### Assessment

Hunter holds detailed working knowledge of APD's proprietary containment-monitoring systems and is believed to retain access to decentralized backups of that technology. Hunter should be considered a serious risk to any Foundation facility making use of APD-derived infrastructure, which, as of this filing, includes a majority of active Sites.

### Instructions

Do not approach. Do not attempt to engage or detain. If Hunter is sighted, secure the immediate area, restrict all APD-derived system access at that location, and contact Internal Security immediately through standard emergency channels.

This bulletin will be updated as new information becomes available.

files.title: SCP-APD-001
files.subtitle: The Quiet Ledger
files.author: Dr. ███████
files.summary: A leather-bound ledger that writes a single line whenever its owner is owed an apology they will never receive.
files.body:
**Item #:** APD-001

**Object Class:** Safe

### Special Containment Procedures

SCP-APD-001 is stored in a standard document safe in Site-19 Archival Wing B. It requires no climate control. Personnel assigned to SCP-APD-001 for research purposes must submit Form APD-7 and may not remove the object from the archival wing.

### Description

SCP-APD-001 is a leather-bound ledger, 14 cm x 20 cm, containing 212 pages of unlined paper. The cover bears no title. When held by a human subject for longer than ninety consecutive seconds, SCP-APD-001 will open to the next blank page and a single line of text will appear in handwriting matching the subject's own, describing an instance in which someone owed the subject an apology they did not receive.

Entries are historically accurate as far as they can be verified. Subjects have reported the experience as "unsettling but not unwelcome." No entry has ever repeated. SCP-APD-001 has not been observed to run out of blank pages despite consistent use since recovery.

### Addendum APD-001-1

SCP-APD-001 was recovered from an estate sale in ████████, acquired by a Foundation front company after a routine sweep flagged unusual buyer interest in the listing. No anomalous activity was reported at the point of sale. Current hypothesis places SCP-APD-001's origin with an unidentified minor reality-bending entity, designation pending.

### Addendum APD-001-2: Redesignation

Following the suspension of APD's exclusive license, items still bearing the SCP-APD- designation are under case-by-case review for standard Foundation renumbering. This item has been reassigned designation **SCP-3902**. This file is retained under its original filing number for continuity of record. See APD-14: FACILITY HISTORY & DESIGNATION PROTOCOL and APD | DELTA-20 for background on the original designation.

directories.description: Administrative paperwork, low-sensitivity incident logs, and orientation material for personnel newly assigned to Site-19.

files.title: APD: FOUNDING & FOUNDATION PARTNERSHIP
files.subtitle: A Corporate History
files.author: APD Corporate Archives
files.summary: How a private engineering firm became the Foundation's most trusted outside contractor, and how APD-14 became its flagship site.
files.body:
## Origins

APD, Anomalous Properties Database, was founded in 2013 as a private systems engineering firm with no formal ties to the Foundation. Its founding engineers specialized in high-integrity data architecture: systems built to hold enormous volumes of sensitive material without losing a byte, without leaking a byte, and without going down.

The Foundation found them almost by accident. Following a catastrophic archival corruption event at a mid-sized Site in 2016, three independent audits failed to recover the lost containment records. APD's proposal was the fourth. It worked in six weeks, and it worked completely.

## The Exclusive License

What began as a single recovery contract became, over the following two years, a full licensing agreement. The Foundation does not typically permit outside vendors anywhere near classified material, but the volume of anomalous data being generated Foundation-wide had begun to outpace the Foundation's own engineering divisions. APD offered a solution the Foundation could not build fast enough internally: containment-monitoring software, predictive breach-detection systems, and the database architecture underlying entire Sites' record-keeping.

In exchange for exclusivity, meaning no other outside contractor would be permitted the access APD was granted, APD accepted the Foundation's full security regime: amnestic-backed nondisclosure agreements for every employee, mandatory cognitohazard-safety certification, and unannounced compliance audits. It was, by outside-vendor standards, an extraordinarily invasive arrangement. APD's leadership signed it without hesitation.

## Growth and APD-14

APD expanded quickly. Individual facilities were designated numerically in the order they were established. APD-14, built to serve Site-19's rapidly growing technical footprint, became the company's flagship location: its largest engineering staff, its primary research campus, and eventually the seat of its executive leadership.

As APD's systems became load-bearing for entire Sites' day-to-day operations, standard Foundation Mobile Task Forces proved poorly suited to responding to failures in hardware and software most of them had never been trained on. With Foundation approval, APD raised its own limited security and technical-response teams: personnel cleared to operate inside active containment environments, but answerable to APD's own chain of command rather than the Foundation's.

By its tenth year of operation, APD held a designation prefix of its own, SCP-APD-, reserved for anomalies catalogued through APD's systems. It is a level of integration rarely extended to anyone outside the Foundation itself.

## Addendum

APD-14 suffered a catastrophic systems loss on 2026-06-18. Details of the incident, and the security bulletin concerning former Site Director Ryu Hunter, are filed separately at Level 4 and Level 1 clearance respectively. Following the suspension of its Foundation license, APD has reoriented its remaining operations more heavily toward its military and splinter-group division; see APD | DELTA-20 for current status. This document has not otherwise been rewritten to reflect those events. It is preserved as it was filed, for the historical record.

files.title: APD | DELTA-20
files.subtitle: Unit History: Field Recovery Division
files.author: Delta-20 Command
files.summary: Formation, notable recoveries, and current status of APD's first field recovery unit.
files.body:
## Formation

APD | Delta-20 was raised in late 2020 as the company's first dedicated field recovery unit, organized under APD's military and splinter-group division rather than under any single facility. Delta-class units answer to APD's central command structure directly. Delta-20 has never been organizationally subordinate to APD-14 or any other site, even where its operational focus has overlapped heavily with Site-19's territory. Delta-class units are recovery and first-response specialists, trained to locate, stabilize, and transport newly detected anomalies before Foundation MTF involvement is required.

Twenty is not, despite appearances, the twentieth Delta-class unit. APD's internal numbering block for Delta units begins at 15, reserved in advance for an expansion that only ever partially materialized. Delta-20 was, in practice, the second Delta unit raised and the first to reach full operational status.

Delta-20 was founded and commanded in its first years by then-Field Commander Ryu Hunter, prior to Hunter's promotion to Site Director of APD-14 in 2022, a rare cross-branch appointment that moved him from APD's tactical side into its facilities administration.

## Structure

Delta-20 operates in three-person recovery cells, with five cells typically rostered at any given time: a technical specialist, a field handler, and a security escort. Cells report to a rotating duty commander rather than a fixed hierarchy, a structure Hunter is on record as having insisted on personally, on the grounds that recovery work rewards initiative more than chain of command.

## Notable Recoveries

Delta-20's first confirmed recovery, in early 2021, was a leather-bound ledger pulled from an estate sale after a Foundation front company flagged unusual buyer interest in the listing. The item wrote a single line of text in the reader's own hand whenever they were owed an apology they would never receive. It was filed as SCP-APD-001, and Delta-20 took its unofficial nickname, "Quiet Ledger," from the case. SCP-APD-001 has since been redesignated SCP-3902 following the suspension of APD's license; the file remains under its original filing number in Site-19 General Records for continuity.

In 2023, a cell recovered a privately owned firearm reported to never miss its intended target and never run out of ammunition, filed as SCP-APD-014 and currently held at APD-14 pending its own designation review.

Delta-20's other recoveries over the following years are filed individually; several remain under the SCP-APD- designation pending case-by-case review.

## Current Status

Following the loss of APD-14 and the suspension of APD's Foundation license, the company has reoriented its remaining operations more heavily toward its military and splinter-group division. With its facility-based engineering and database business no longer active under Foundation authorization, Delta-20 and units like it have become more central to APD's day-to-day operation, not less, even as some members are reassigned pending clearance re-verification in light of Hunter's fugitive status. Others have not been accounted for since 2026-06-18.

files.title: APD-14: FACILITY HISTORY & DESIGNATION PROTOCOL
files.subtitle: Site Establishment and the SCP-APD Prefix
files.author: APD Corporate Archives
files.summary: How APD-14 came to be built beside Site-19, and the exact criteria behind the SCP-APD designation prefix.
files.body:
## Site Selection

When APD's engineering contracts with Site-19 outgrew what could be managed through rotating on-site contractors, the company began looking for a permanent presence near the Site itself. Site-19 was, by 2018, already one of the Foundation's largest and busiest installations, and its own engineering divisions could not keep pace with growing archival and containment-monitoring demand. Ground broke on APD-14 in 2019, chosen specifically for its proximity to Site-19's perimeter.

APD-14 became operational in 2020. What began as a technical annex, server halls, a monitoring floor, and a small engineering staff, grew within two years into APD's largest facility company-wide: research labs, a central systems core, personnel housing, and eventually its executive offices.

## Splinter and Military Groups

Standard Foundation Mobile Task Forces were trained for anomaly containment and recovery, not for APD's proprietary detection and monitoring hardware. As anomalous activity in the Site-19 operational area outpaced what Foundation MTFs could be trained to handle on APD's systems, the Foundation authorized APD to raise its own specialized units under a separate military and splinter-group division: personnel answerable to APD's central command rather than to any single facility, cleared and equipped to operate in the field alongside, or independently of, Foundation MTFs.

These units, organized under a letter-and-number convention similar to standard MTF designation, are a distinct branch of APD as a company, not subordinate to APD-14 or any other site, even when their operational focus is centered on Site-19's territory. The first and most prominent of these, APD | Delta-20, is documented separately (Level 3 clearance).

## The SCP-APD Designation Protocol

In 2021, following a string of successful independent recoveries, the Foundation granted APD a standing designation block: SCP-APD-. This was not, as sometimes assumed internally, a blanket honor for anything APD touched. The criteria were narrow and specific, and both had to be met:

1. The anomaly was discovered or recovered directly by an APD-affiliated Splinter or Military unit, not a standard Foundation MTF.
2. Its primary containment was established at either Site-19 or APD-14, not transferred elsewhere prior to designation.

An anomaly recovered by a Foundation MTF, or one transferred out of the Site-19 or APD-14 pair before containment was finalized, retained standard Foundation numbering regardless of any later APD technical involvement in its ongoing monitoring. The distinction mattered enormously to the people doing the recovering. A standard Foundation number meant the item belonged to the Foundation. An SCP-APD number meant the field team could point to something on the shelf and say, we found that.

## Addendum

Following the suspension of APD's exclusive license in June 2026, items still bearing the SCP-APD- designation are under case-by-case review for standard Foundation renumbering. See the file for former SCP-APD-001, now SCP-3902, filed in Site-19 General Records.

files.title: INCIDENT REPORT: LOSS OF APD-14
files.subtitle: Internal Security Division, Post-Incident Review
files.author: Internal Security Division
files.summary: Access denied at your current clearance. This entry cannot be overridden.
files.body:
### Summary

On 2026-06-18, APD-14 suffered the total loss of its central systems core following the unauthorized activation of a facility-wide self-destruct protocol. Three sitting members of the Overseer Council, present at APD-14 for a scheduled asset review, were killed during the resulting evacuation failure. The individual responsible, former APD-14 Site Director Ryu Hunter, remains at large.

### Background

The self-destruct protocol activated was not a Foundation system. It was built into APD-14's original architecture by APD itself, designed to destroy proprietary systems and data beyond recovery in the event of a hostile seizure attempt by a third party such as the Global Occult Coalition. It had never been triggered outside of testing. It was not designed with the Foundation itself as a threat model.

The "asset review" scheduled for 2026-06-18 was, per subsequently recovered internal correspondence, the first formal session of a Council-ordered transition: the dissolution of APD's exclusive license and the direct absorption of its proprietary systems into Foundation infrastructure, staff retained at the Council's discretion. Site Director Hunter was not informed of this in advance through official channels.

### Sequence of Events

Recovered logs place Hunter in the central systems core roughly forty minutes before the review was scheduled to begin. ████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████. The self-destruct sequence began at approximately 14:02 local time.

Evacuation protocols for the wing hosting the review were not initiated by facility systems. Security footage from the two adjoining corridors was among the data destroyed. What can be confirmed: three Council members present for the review, designated O5-███, O5-████, and O5-█████, did not leave the wing before the systems core detonated. Their deaths are not attributed to the blast itself.

### Status of Ryu Hunter

Hunter left APD-14 by unknown means prior to the facility's emergency lockdown and has not been located. A Foundation-wide security bulletin has been issued at Level 1 clearance (see FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT). Hunter is believed to retain access to decentralized backups of APD's proprietary systems and should be considered both armed and technically capable of compromising APD-derived infrastructure elsewhere.

### Addendum

The exclusive license between the Foundation and APD is, as of this filing, suspended pending full review. APD-14 remains standing under Internal Security oversight. This archive is what could be recovered.

directories.description: Founding records, partnership history, and post-incident review materials concerning APD and the APD-14 facility.

files.title: CLASS-D PERSONNEL LOG D-0231
files.subtitle: Standard Rotation Record
files.author: Class-D Management Office
files.summary: Standard rotation and assignment log. Intake, task history, and standard monthly amnesticization schedule.
files.body:
**Designation:** D-0231

**Intake Source:** Standard external channel

**Status:** Active, current rotation

### Assignment History

D-0231 has completed three low-risk cataloguing tasks in Archival Wing B under supervised conditions. No anomalous exposure incidents on record. Scheduled for standard monthly amnesticization per policy.

### Notes

Routine entry, filed for record-keeping completeness only.

files.title: PERSONNEL FILE: KIRIKO
files.subtitle: Field Intelligence Operative | Formerly SCP-6714
files.author: Human Resources, Intelligence Agency
files.summary: Former contained anomaly turned Foundation intelligence asset. Full record includes operational history and biography.
files.body:
## TITLE I. Operational Information

**Serial #:** 847-206-913

**Clearance:** Level 3 (Secret)

**Classification:** Anomalous Personnel, Field Intelligence Operative

**Secondary Clearance:** Containment-Assist Authorization (Conditional, see Title VI)

**SCiPnet Handle:** kiriko.ia06

**Primary Department:** Intelligence Agency

**Rank:** Field Intelligence Operative, Grade II

**Sub-Groups:** None

**Sub-Group Rank:** N/A

**Assigned Task Force(s):** None (standing). Holds Honorary Auxiliary status with MTF Epsilon-11 "Nine-Tailed Fox" for the sole purpose of the containment-assist clause described in Title VI. This status carries no departmental authority and does not constitute reassignment.

**Specialization:** Long-duration deep-cover infiltration and disguise operations, informed by approximately one thousand years of continuous, lived exposure to shifting human societies, languages, and customs, an asset the Intelligence Agency has been unable to replicate through any other means.

**Previous Ranks:** Contained Anomalous Asset (SCP-APD-006, 2022 to 2026; redesignated SCP-6714, 2026)

**Supervisors:** Handler of record withheld at operative's own request; case oversight retained by Intelligence Agency Directorate.

**Outlook:** Exceptional. Kiriko's placement is considered one of the Intelligence Agency's most valuable acquisitions of the decade, though the Agency notes internally that this value was purchased at a cost it did not pay.

---

## TITLE II. Demographic Information

**Full Name:** Kiriko (surname unrecorded; see Biography)

**Known Alias(es):** SCP-APD-006, SCP-6714, "Fox-6" (operational callsign, MTF Epsilon-11 honorary designation)

**Date of Birth:** Unrecorded. Foundation estimate places Kiriko's emergence at approximately 1000 to 1100 CE, based on standard kitsune tail-development timelines and independent corroboration from period-consistent knowledge displayed during early debriefing.

**Place of Birth:** Honshu, Japan (specific region unrecorded)

**Race:** Kitsune (Anomalous)

**Gender:** Female-presenting. Kitsune folklore and Kiriko's own testimony both indicate the species does not hold fixed human categories of sex or gender to be especially meaningful.

**Nationality:** None. Kiriko predates the modern Japanese state by several centuries and has never held or sought citizenship anywhere. Formally classified as a Foundation Ward.

**Ethnicity:** N/A (Anomalous Species)

**Home Site:** Site-19

**Place of Residence:** Site-19, Staff Wing C, Unit 14

**Major Scars:** Extensive scarring along the left flank and shoulder, consistent with claw injuries, sustained during engagement with SCP-953. A faded, roughly circular scar on the inside of the left forearm, of anomalous origin, believed related to prior captivity.

**Tattoo/Markings:** A small alphanumeric brand on the back of the neck, partially obscured by hair, matching inventory markings later associated with the trafficking operation known internally as Verdigris Exotics. Kiriko has declined all offers of cosmetic removal.

**Eyewear:** None required. Kiriko's natural low-light vision exceeds standard-issue night vision equipment.

**Extra:** Fur and hair present in an uneven silver-and-green pattern, the green concentrated at the roots and tail-tips. This is not natural kitsune coloration. It is the permanent result of anomalous forced pigmentation and has not reverted despite years in both prior APD and current Foundation custody. Kiriko has been offered cosmetic correction on four occasions and has declined each time.

---

## Appearance Description

**Physical Description:** Kiriko presents as a woman in early adulthood by human visual standards, though this bears no relation to her true age. She stands approximately 5'6", with a slight, wiry build shaped more by centuries of practiced movement than by any conventional training regimen. Nine tails, silver at the base and darkening to a mottled, unnatural green toward the tips, are typically kept bound and concealed beneath clothing or a long coat during field operations, released only at will. Fox ears replace human ears entirely. Kiriko relies on a minor anomalous property allowing her to render both imperceptible to unfocused observation without physically altering them, rather than any illusion of a fully human appearance. Her eyes are a pale amber-gold, with vertically slit pupils in low light.

**Attire Description:** Field attire consists of a modified E-11 tactical vest, tailored to accommodate her tails, worn over dark civilian-cut clothing suited to blending into an urban environment on short notice. The vest, along with the sidearm and rifle described in Title VI, previously belonged to Lieutenant Marcus Kade. Kiriko has had none of it reissued in her own name. Foundation Quartermaster records still list all three items under Kade's service number, at Kiriko's specific and repeated request.

---

## TITLE III. Medical & Psychological Record

**Sex:** Female (see Gender, Title II)

**Hair Color:** Silver, with green discoloration (anomalous, non-reverting)

**Height:** 5'6" (168 cm)

**Eye Color:** Amber-gold

**Weight:** 128 lbs (58 kg)

**Ethnicity:** N/A

**Blood Type:** Not applicable in the conventional sense. Kiriko's physiology supports standard transfusion medicine using type O-negative in emergencies, per Site-19 Medical.

**Prescriptions:** None

**Allergies:** None recorded

**Conditions:** None formally recorded. Site-19 Medical flags recurring sleep disturbance in the months following the events described in the Biography; this has not been formally diagnosed at Kiriko's request and is monitored informally by her handler instead.

**Augments:** None

**Last Updated:** 2027-03-15

**Abstract:** Kiriko presents as reserved, watchful, and unfailingly polite to a degree several staff have described as "performed," in the sense of a habit maintained long after its original purpose stopped mattering. She warms slowly and, once she does, is fiercely loyal, to a fault her handlers have flagged more than once. She does not discuss her captivity under Verdigris Exotics unless directly and privately asked, and has never discussed it in detail with more than one person at Site-19.

**Virtues:** Loyalty, patience, precision, an old-fashioned sense of debt and obligation that Foundation psychological staff have both praised and worried over in the same report.

**Vices:** A tendency to take on risk that is not hers to take, particularly where it might spare someone else from taking it instead. Difficulty accepting help.

**Likes:** Quiet rooms. Green tea, an old habit predating the Foundation by centuries. The sound of a facility at night, after most personnel have gone to bed.

**Dislikes:** Being described as "recovered" or "healed." Cages of any kind, including ordinary elevators, though she rides them without comment. Being asked to justify her own value.

**Psychiatric Conditions:** None formally diagnosed. Staff psychologist notes indicate symptoms consistent with prolonged captivity trauma and complicated grief, both declined for formal diagnosis at the subject's request, a request Site-19's Ethics Committee has elected to honor given her legal status as personnel rather than a contained subject.

**Psychiatrist's Orders:** Do not bring up Lieutenant Kade by name in a debrief setting; allow Kiriko to raise the subject herself. Do not use the word "rescued." Do not schedule field assignments during the week of November 3rd without her explicit sign-off.

---

## TITLE IV. Legal & Ethical Information

**Injuries in Service:** Multiple lacerations to the left flank and shoulder sustained in direct combat with SCP-953 on 2026-11-03, requiring immediate surgical intervention. Full recovery was achieved within three weeks, attributed in part to standard kitsune healing rates, which Site-19 Medical notes are elevated but explicitly not instantaneous or anomalous in the manner Foundation policy would otherwise restrict.

**Security Encounters:** One unauthorized egress from containment during the Site-19 cascade event of 2026-11-03, immediately followed by voluntary cooperation with MTF Epsilon-11 field operations. No disciplinary action was pursued; see Biography.

**Ethical/Legal Violations:** None. The Ethics Committee's sole formal action regarding Kiriko was retroactive: a unanimous vote on 2026-12-11 to classify her prior captivity, rather than any of her own conduct, as the matter warranting review.

**Captain's Orders:** Standing order from MTF Epsilon-11 command, dated 2026-11-04: any member of Epsilon-11 present at Site-19 is to treat Kiriko's judgment on containment matters as equivalent to a field agent's, pending and now superseding formal reclassification. Order has not been rescinded.

---

## TITLE V. Biographical Information

**Relatives:** None known. Kiriko has stated, on the one occasion she discussed it, that any kitsune she once called kin predate Foundation record-keeping entirely and are presumed either dispersed or dead.

**Criminal Record:** None, in any jurisdiction, Foundation or civil.

**Relations:** Lieutenant Marcus Kade, MTF Epsilon-11 (deceased, 2026-11-03; see Biography). Captain Elena Osei, MTF Epsilon-11 (current primary point of contact). Dr. Naomi Farrow, Intelligence Agency (handler of record, named here despite Title I's withholding, at Kiriko's own preference for informal contexts).

**Education:** None in any formal institutional sense. Extensive self-directed knowledge of history, multiple human languages (Kiriko is conversationally fluent in no fewer than eleven, by her own count, and reads several dead ones), and applied folklore, all acquired through lived experience rather than instruction.

**Experience:** Approximately one thousand years of direct, continuous observation of human society, a qualification the Intelligence Agency has noted, without irony, no current staff member can compete with.

**Former Job:** Contained anomalous asset, APD-14 (2022 to 2026); contained anomalous asset, Site-19 (2026).

### ADDENDUM.CODENAME/II: Biography

« BEGIN TRANSCRIPT »

Kiriko does not remember a childhood in any sense a human researcher could file under that heading. What she has offered, across several long debriefs conducted more like conversations than interrogations, is this: she remembers Honshu before it had that name. She remembers shrines that no longer stand, and the particular quality of silence in a pine forest before dawn, and being warned, by kitsune older than her who are themselves long gone, that a fox with nine tails should never let itself be seen twice by the same human. She kept that rule for the better part of a millennium. It did not save her.

The organization that found her called itself, on paper, Verdigris Exotics, an unremarkable import-export front operating out of a series of warehouses that moved every few years to stay ahead of anyone looking too closely. Its actual business was the acquisition, alteration, and sale of anomalous creatures to private collectors willing to pay extraordinary sums for something no one else in the world could own. A nine-tailed kitsune, silver-coated and demonstrably ancient, was already a once-in-a-generation acquisition by the standards of that market. Verdigris wanted more than that. Internal documents recovered during the Delta-20 raid indicate the organization had begun a program of forced anomalous pigmentation, altering captured kitsune into colors folklore had never recorded, on the theory that a buyer would pay more for something that looked like nothing that had ever existed before. Kiriko was mid-treatment, silver bleeding into an unnatural green from the roots outward, when APD | Delta-20 breached the facility.

She has described the years of her captivity in detail exactly once, to exactly one person, and it is not reproduced here out of respect for that limitation. What is on record is simpler: Delta-20 cleared the facility room by room, and when a cell door built for something considerably less dangerous than a nine-tailed kitsune was found still welded shut, standard procedure called for it to be flagged for later disposal rather than opened blind. A member of the recovery team, against standard procedure, opened it anyway. Kiriko did not attack. She asked, in Japanese first and then in accented but functional English, whether the warehouse was on fire, because she could smell smoke and wanted to know if she should be afraid of that too, on top of everything else. It was not on fire. Delta-20 had simply started their breach with incendiary charges on the perimeter fence.

APD-14 catalogued her as SCP-APD-006 in the spring of 2022. Her four subsequent years in APD custody were, by her own account, the first genuinely stable period of her existence in longer than she could reliably measure. She was not free, and she has said she understood why she could not be, even at the time. But she was fed, spoken to as a person rather than an asset, and given, eventually, access to books, which she consumed at a rate that alarmed APD's research staff before it began to please them. When APD's license was suspended in June of 2026 and its remaining contained assets began transferring to direct Foundation custody, Kiriko's file moved to Site-19 along with roughly a dozen others. She has since said, more than once, that she expected the transfer to be a step backward. It was not, though not for any reason she could have predicted.

Site-19's standing containment assignment for SCP-6714, as she was redesignated upon Foundation intake, fell, by administrative accident rather than design, under the oversight rotation of MTF Epsilon-11, the unit informally and, everyone involved agrees, extremely unfortunately nicknamed "Nine-Tailed Fox" decades before anyone at Site-19 had ever heard of Kiriko. Lieutenant Marcus Kade, then thirty-four, drew the rotation. Site-19 personnel present that day describe his first words to her, through the observation glass, as "so which one of us is the joke," which Kiriko has confirmed she found funny enough to actually laugh out loud, for what researchers on duty noted may have been the first time on record.

What followed was not, contrary to what the phrase "developed a relationship" might suggest to an outside reader, anything resembling special treatment. Kade visited during his own off-duty hours, logged every visit per protocol, and answered the questions Kiriko asked with the same directness he would have used with a new recruit. She wanted to know, first, why the doors worked the way they did. Then why some doors had two locks and some had four. Then, eventually and more pointedly, what would happen, procedurally, if a containment cell like hers ever failed during an active breach elsewhere in the facility, whether she would be protected, evacuated, or simply left. It was this last question, raised after a minor and unrelated breach three cells down in August of 2026, that Kade brought to his superiors and to Site-19's research staff directly, requesting formal authorization to answer it honestly rather than deflect it. Authorization was granted, on the condition the sessions be logged and reviewed. Neither side ever seemed to mind the paperwork.

By November, four months into an arrangement that had by then quietly stopped being purely procedural on either side, Site-19 experienced what internal Foundation communications still refer to only as the November Cascade. A single compromised containment cell, three sublevels below Kiriko's own, failed catastrophically at 02:14 on the 3rd. The cell held SCP-953, an entity of Korean origin publicly filed as a nine-tailed kumiho and privately regarded by every kitsune the Foundation has ever debriefed, Kiriko included, as something closer to a natural predator than a peer. Kumiho and kitsune are not merely different species by Foundation taxonomy. They are, in the specific words Kiriko herself used during her post-incident debrief, old enemies who have simply never stopped being at war, and SCP-953's escape triggered five further sympathetic containment failures across Site-19's lower levels within the hour, each response drawing further security resources away from the rest of the facility until Site command declared a Level 5 cascade and authorized whatever measures were necessary to prevent a total loss event.

Kiriko's own cell failed at 02:51, a secondary casualty of power redistribution rather than any action of her own. What she has stated, and what MTF Epsilon-11's after-action report independently corroborates from three separate accounts, is that she did not attempt to flee the facility. She went looking for Kade.

She found him instead already fighting SCP-953 alongside two other members of Epsilon-11, in a service corridor neither of them had any tactical reason to be defending except that it led, eventually, to Kiriko's own containment wing. The account every surviving witness agrees on is this: SCP-953, on sighting Kiriko, disengaged from the human combatants entirely and went for her instead, and what began as a firefight became, within seconds, something closer to what the Epsilon-11 after-action report calls, without any apparent irony, "a duel." Two nine-tailed entities, one silver-and-green and one the report describes only as "wrong to look at directly for more than a few seconds," met in the open for what was likely the first time either had encountered its own kind, hostile or otherwise, in centuries. It was not a clean fight. Kade was struck attempting to pull Kiriko clear of an opening strike she did not see coming, a wound to the abdomen that would prove, though no one recognized it in the moment, to already be fatal.

The battle that followed lasted, per surviving footage, six minutes. SCP-953 broke off and fled deeper into the facility once it became clear it would not win outright, a retreat Kiriko has described with open, uncomplicated fury as the one part of that night she has not made peace with. What she noticed only once the corridor went quiet was that Kade was gone.

He had not gone far. Facility logs place him at the manual override terminal for Site-19's Level 5 scuttle protocol, a fail-deadly nuclear contingency automatically initiated forty minutes into any uncontained Level 5 cascade and designed expressly to require a human hand at that specific terminal to abort it, so that no automated system could ever be tricked or compromised into stopping it early. Kade had removed his vest, sidearm, and rifle before making the walk, later found stacked neatly on the corridor floor precisely where Kiriko would pass them, and had sealed the blast door behind him manually rather than let it lock on its own, specifically, investigators concluded, so that Kiriko could not follow him through before it closed. She tried anyway. She did not reach it in time.

What the terminal logs show is a manual abort sequence entered in full, correctly, on the first attempt, at 03:34, six minutes before the automatic detonation window would have closed permanently and forty minutes after Kade had already taken a wound Site-19 Medical would later determine no field intervention could have saved him from. He did not radio for help. He did not wait to be found. Kiriko reached the terminal room eleven minutes later, having spent those eleven minutes doing exactly what she had once idly asked him to explain to her: working, unit by unit, room by room, with the two other members of Epsilon-11 still standing, helping re-secure or reassess three separate compromised containment cells along the way, because it was, by then, simply the next necessary thing to do, and she was, by then, someone who knew how. She found Kade seated against the terminal console, already gone, his hand still resting on the panel he had used to save the facility that had, six months earlier, been holding her prisoner in it.

There is no clean way to file what happened afterward, so this document will not attempt one. Kiriko was held for questioning for eleven days, a length of time Internal Security has since acknowledged, in writing, as longer than the circumstances warranted. Site-19's containment posture toward her was formally tightened and then, within the month, formally reversed entirely, once it became clear to every relevant authority, up to and including members of the O5 Council briefed directly on the incident, that the Foundation had, by pure administrative accident, ended up in possession of something considerably more valuable than a contained anomaly: a being of extraordinary age, extraordinary patience, and now-demonstrated loyalty, who had just spent a night proving, at cost, that she would rather help than run. The Intelligence Agency's formal request for her transfer was approved on 2027-01-09. She accepted it on the one condition she asked for, granted without argument: that she never be asked to work anywhere else.

She still keeps Kade's rifle. She has never asked for one of her own.

« END TRANSCRIPT »

---

## TITLE VI. Extra Information

**Weaponry & Equipment:** One HK416D carbine, serial number retained under Lt. M. Kade's original service record at Kiriko's request. One modified E-11 tactical vest, likewise retained under Kade's designation. Standard Foundation-issue sidearm, held but rarely carried.

**Emergency Anomalous Protocols:** In the event Kiriko is compromised, incapacitated by hostile anomalous influence, or otherwise required to be subdued, standard containment teams are advised that her primary anomalous properties (illusory concealment and minor elemental manifestation) are visual and thermal in nature and do not affect physical restraint. Silver-plated restraints are recommended per longstanding, if only partially substantiated, kitsune folklore, and have shown modest additional effect in controlled testing. Lethal force remains authorized only as an absolute last resort and requires sign-off from two of the following: Site-19 Command, MTF Epsilon-11 Command, or the Intelligence Agency Directorate. No single authority may authorize it alone.

**Anomalous Description:** Kiriko possesses four documented anomalous properties, consistent with standard kitsune physiology and not the product of any known external augmentation beyond the pigmentation damage described in Title II. (1) Selective visual concealment of ears and tails, sustainable indefinitely at rest and for approximately six hours under active use before requiring recovery. (2) Minor pyrokinetic manifestation ("kitsune-bi"), producing cool, non-igniting spectral flame usable for signaling, minor illumination, or short-range deterrence; it has never been observed to cause structural damage or serious burns in any recorded incident. (3) Sensory acuity significantly exceeding baseline human norms across smell and low-light vision. (4) Natural biological longevity correlated with tail count, currently nine, the theoretical maximum recorded in any Foundation-verified case. This is not immortality. Kiriko has stated plainly, when asked, that she does not believe herself unable to die, only unlikely to have died yet.

**Paratechnology:** None issued.

**Addendum:** Site-19's Chapel of the Fallen lists Lieutenant Marcus Kade among its dead. Beneath his name, in a hand that is not Foundation standard-issue and that no one on staff has taken any action to remove, someone has added seven words: he left the door open anyway.

---

## NOTICE

Effective immediately following the conclusion of all proceedings related to the events described above, Kiriko is to be transferred to another site, destination withheld pending final approval, to eliminate any perceived or actual bias arising from her continued proximity to Site-19 and to the surviving members of MTF Epsilon-11. This is a personnel and operational-integrity decision made jointly by the Intelligence Agency Directorate and Internal Security, and is not, and should not be construed as, any judgment on Kiriko's conduct.

Kiriko has not contested the order. She has requested, and been granted, standing authorization to return to Site-19's Chapel of the Fallen once annually, escorted, on the anniversary of the November Cascade. This is the only exception made to the transfer.

Title II of this record will be updated to reflect Kiriko's new Home Site upon completion of the transfer.

files.title: PERSONNEL FILE: RYU HUNTER
files.subtitle: Former Site Director, APD-14
files.author: Human Resources, APD-14
files.summary: Former Site Director for APD-14. Status: fugitive. See FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT.
files.body:
**Designation:** Site Director (TERMINATED)

**Assignment:** APD-14

**Status:** FUGITIVE. See FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT.

### Summary

Ryu Hunter served as Site Director of APD-14 from 2022 until 2026-06-18, promoted from field command of APD | Delta-20, overseeing archival operations, containment logistics, and the day-to-day welfare of APD's technical staff. Performance reviews across that period were, without exception, strongly positive: a preference for measured protocol enforcement over reactionary discipline, and a hands-on approach to onboarding new researchers personally rather than delegating orientation to subordinates.

This record is retained for historical accuracy. It should not be read as inconsistent with the events of 2026-06-18. See INCIDENT REPORT: LOSS OF APD-14 (Level 4 clearance) for the full account.

### Notes

Maintained parallel administrative ties to several independent archival and myth-cataloguing projects outside the standard APD network. Those projects are presently under review to determine whether any share infrastructure, funding, or personnel with APD, and whether any pose a continuity risk given Hunter's fugitive status.

Whereabouts unknown. Believed to retain access to decentralized backups of APD proprietary systems.

directories.description: Service records for APD personnel currently or formerly assigned to Site APD-14.
Training simulations and field exercises maintained by APD engineering staff.
